other_observers: none; I found this one near where a juvenile Long-tailed Jaeger had flown from.
specific_location: Antero Reservoir, South Side, in cove near the farthest fishing point, on the other side of the point than the campground.
description: This was a phalarope by coloration, behavior, and bill shape and length.   A gray phalorope with the remanent patches on throat and sides of neck of buffy juvenile plumage. The bill, for a phalarope, was thick at the base and blunt-tipped and dark and about the same length as the width of the head. The face was white with a black mask and black on the crown. The nape was white with just a little remaining buffy coloration from juvenile plumage. The light gray back was without any dark streaks. The rump (photo) was whitish. The gray wing has a very wide white streak especially wide on the secondaries (photo). The ventral surface was whitish except for a noticeably buffy throat patch. The bird rode high in the water.Behaviors: Rapidly feeding on hatching midges fairly close to shore. Picked midges from the surface but also from the air. Spread wings once but did not fly.Call: non heardPlumage: juvenile
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
